title: strumaria tenella subsp. tenella
intro: Strumaria tenella subsp. tenella, also known as the yellow grass, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae, native to the southern parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relief from respiratory issues and skin conditions, as well as being used to treat fever and digestive problems. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Strumaria tenella subsp. tenella have been made into infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
